subject: [PATCH v6 0/2] firmware_loader: allow firmware_class.path to take multiple paths

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: Greg pointed out that the patch is still experimental and may have issues, specifically mentioning potential problems with bash scripts. He suggested looking at a different patchset for reference.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Apr 01, 2026 at 09:43:26AM -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> This is something Michal had asked for last year, and I just got around
> to implementing. This version is just a minor cleanup for
> reviewability's sake. I also renamed the search= option to search_path=.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---

While it is still experimental, and might not be fully correct, you
might want to look here:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260401-fw-path-v6-0-4ebe70441839%40kernel.org
to see a few potential issues for this patchset (notibly at least the
bash stuff)

thanks,

greg k-h

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch series allows the firmware_class.path to take multiple paths, addressing a long-standing request from Michal. The changes include adding fw_search_unescape() for better readability, renaming search= to search_path=, and increasing the length of the search string from 256 to 4096.

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch series allows the firmware_class.path to take multiple paths, addressing a long-standing request from Michal. The changes include adding fw_search_unescape() for better readability, renaming search= to search_path=, and increasing the length of the search string from 256 to 4096.
